findings.claim: Furthermore, we found that mutual inhibition among SOM interneurons was strong and more prevalent than previously thought, while mutual inhibition among PV interneurons was minimal.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Furthermore, we found that mutual inhibition among SOM interneurons was strong and more prevalent than previously thought, while mutual inhibition among PV interneurons was minimal.

findings.claim: We demonstrate that parvalbumin neurons in the dentate molecular layer express PTHLH, an AAC marker, and exhibit morphology characteristic of AACs.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We demonstrate that parvalbumin neurons in the dentate molecular layer express PTHLH, an AAC marker, and exhibit morphology characteristic of AACs.

findings.claim: We found that fast-spiking interneurons exhibited robust gamma-frequency firing, while excitatory neurons engaged in gamma cycle skipping.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We found that fast-spiking interneurons exhibited robust gamma-frequency firing, while excitatory neurons engaged in gamma cycle skipping.
findings.effect_size: 140 Hz) play a fundamental role in neural coordination and cognitive functions in the medial entorhinal cortex (mEC)
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Gamma oscillations (40-140 Hz) play a fundamental role in neural coordination and cognitive functions in the medial entorhinal cortex (mEC).

findings.claim: Together, these results delineate the roles for SST and PV neurons in active synaptic integration.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Together, these results delineate the roles for SST and PV neurons in active synaptic integration.

findings.claim: Our findings indicate that CCK+ interneurons contribute to MD-evoked inhibition in PL, revealing a mechanism by which cannabinoids can modulate MD-PFC communication.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Our findings indicate that CCK+ interneurons contribute to MD-evoked inhibition in PL, revealing a mechanism by which cannabinoids can modulate MD-PFC communication.

findings.claim: Our results show that most GABAergic axonal varicosities contacting the perisomatic region of superficial (layer 2/3) and deep (layer 5) pyramidal cells express parvalbumin (PV) or cannabinoid receptor type 1 (CB1).
findings.claim_source_sentence: Our results show that most GABAergic axonal varicosities contacting the perisomatic region of superficial (layer 2/3) and deep (layer 5) pyramidal cells express parvalbumin (PV) or cannabinoid receptor type 1 (CB1).

findings.claim: We found that the decay of GABAAR-IPSCs, possibly including those from parvalbumin-positive GABA neurons, shortened by prepubertal age, while their amplitude increased until the peripubertal period.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We found that the decay of GABAAR-IPSCs, possibly including those from parvalbumin-positive GABA neurons, shortened by prepubertal age, while their amplitude increased until the peripubertal period.

findings.claim: Light- and electron microscopy analyses revealed pvBC axons innervating their own soma and proximal dendrites.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Light- and electron microscopy analyses revealed pvBC axons innervating their own soma and proximal dendrites.

findings.claim: These results suggest that subsets of parvalbumin interneurons are components of specific cortical networks and that perisomatic inhibition contributes to the generation of precise response properties.
findings.claim_source_sentence: These results suggest that subsets of parvalbumin interneurons are components of specific cortical networks and that perisomatic inhibition contributes to the generation of precise response properties.

findings.claim: Our results support the idea that chandelier cells depolarize pyramidal neurons and could potentially have an excitatory effect on the network at rest.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Our results support the idea that chandelier cells depolarize pyramidal neurons and could potentially have an excitatory effect on the network at rest.

findings.claim: We demonstrate that layer 5 intra- (L5 IT) and extra-telencephalic (L5 ET) neurons express unique transcriptional programs, allowing them to shape parvalbumin- (PV+) and cholecystokinin-positive (CCK+) interneuron wiring.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We demonstrate that layer 5 intra- (L5 IT) and extra-telencephalic (L5 ET) neurons express unique transcriptional programs, allowing them to shape parvalbumin- (PV+) and cholecystokinin-positive (CCK+) interneuron wiring.

findings.claim: Combining intracellular and extracellular recordings from pyramidal cells and interneurons, we confirm that inhibitory signals generated by basket cells can be recorded extracellularly, but our results suggest that, during spontaneous activity, eIPSPs are mostly confined to the CA3 rather than CA1 r
findings.claim_source_sentence: Combining intracellular and extracellular recordings from pyramidal cells and interneurons, we confirm that inhibitory signals generated by basket cells can be recorded extracellularly, but our results suggest that, during spontaneous activity, eIPSPs are mostly confined to the CA3 rather than CA1 region.
findings.effect_size: 10 ms time scale, within which neurons recruit each other through recurrent collaterals and trigger powerful feedback inhibition
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Analysis of the temporal dynamics of spike discharges relative to eIPSPs suggests significant but moderate recruitment of excitatory and inhibitory neurons within the CA3 network on a 10 ms time scale, within which neurons recruit each other through recurrent collaterals and trigger powerful feedback inhibition.

findings.claim: Here we show that this equilibrium can be unlocked by increased activity of layer 5 pyramidal neurons of the mouse neocortex.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here we show that this equilibrium can be unlocked by increased activity of layer 5 pyramidal neurons of the mouse neocortex.

findings.claim: Exposure to nociceptive heat stimulation significantly increased the amount of pain-related escape behavior, and PVN activation by optogenetics did not significantly decrease pain-related behavior.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Exposure to nociceptive heat stimulation significantly increased the amount of pain-related escape behavior, and PVN activation by optogenetics did not significantly decrease pain-related behavior.
findings.effect_size: 60 mV membrane potential of PNs
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Long-term potentiation was induced by either the -45 or -60 mV membrane potential of PNs.

findings.claim: Picrotoxin increased firing and GABA decreased firing in WT PV interneurons but not in cKO PV interneurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Picrotoxin increased firing and GABA decreased firing in WT PV interneurons but not in cKO PV interneurons.

findings.claim: In contrast to PV+ neurons in mouse visual cortex, which are broadly tuned for orientation, we found that auditory cortical PV+ neurons were well tuned for frequency, although very tightly tuned PV+ cells were uncommon.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In contrast to PV+ neurons in mouse visual cortex, which are broadly tuned for orientation, we found that auditory cortical PV+ neurons were well tuned for frequency, although very tightly tuned PV+ cells were uncommon.
findings.effect_size: 20% of the cortical population and can be separated into numerous subclasses
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Cortical inhibition is provided by local GABAergic interneurons, which comprise 10-20% of the cortical population and can be separated into numerous subclasses.

findings.claim: Acute in vivo silencing of the contralateral cortex generally increased spontaneous firing across cortical layers and linearly transformed responses to pure tones via both divisive and additive operations.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Acute in vivo silencing of the contralateral cortex generally increased spontaneous firing across cortical layers and linearly transformed responses to pure tones via both divisive and additive operations.

findings.claim: In the stargazer mouse model of absence epilepsy, reduced FFI is believed to contribute to absence seizure genesis as there is a specific loss of excitatory α-amino-3-hydroxy-5-methyl-4-isoxazolepropionic acid receptors (AMPARs) at synaptic inputs to PV+ interneurons within the CTC network.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In the stargazer mouse model of absence epilepsy, reduced FFI is believed to contribute to absence seizure genesis as there is a specific loss of excitatory α-amino-3-hydroxy-5-methyl-4-isoxazolepropionic acid receptors (AMPARs) at synaptic inputs to PV+ interneurons within the CTC network.

findings.claim: Here we show in mice that inputs from the MD drive disynaptic feedforward inhibition in the dorsal anterior cingulate cortex (dACC) subregion of the mPFC.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here we show in mice that inputs from the MD drive disynaptic feedforward inhibition in the dorsal anterior cingulate cortex (dACC) subregion of the mPFC.

findings.claim: Single PV interneurons could trigger very large (~ 1 nA) feedback excitatory postsynaptic currents (fbEPSCs) suggesting that these neurons are heavily reciprocally connected to local glutamatergic principal cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Single PV interneurons could trigger very large (~ 1 nA) feedback excitatory postsynaptic currents (fbEPSCs) suggesting that these neurons are heavily reciprocally connected to local glutamatergic principal cells.

findings.claim: These connectivity characteristics can promote synchronous firing in the local networks of CPn cells and firing of some CCS cells by anode-break excitation.
findings.claim_source_sentence: These connectivity characteristics can promote synchronous firing in the local networks of CPn cells and firing of some CCS cells by anode-break excitation.

findings.claim: We found that PVNs act like a volume knob in the mouse auditory cortex (ACtx), bi-directionally adjusting neural and perceptual sensitivity to sound level over a 20 dB range.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We found that PVNs act like a volume knob in the mouse auditory cortex (ACtx), bi-directionally adjusting neural and perceptual sensitivity to sound level over a 20 dB range.
findings.effect_size: 40 Hz-but not at 1 or 70 Hz-sustainably dampened ACtx sound responsiveness, enhanced feedforward inhibition, and behaviorally desensitized mice to loudness
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: PVN-based gain adjustments were "sticky," such that a single bout of PVN activation at 40 Hz-but not at 1 or 70 Hz-sustainably dampened ACtx sound responsiveness, enhanced feedforward inhibition, and behaviorally desensitized mice to loudness.

findings.claim: We found that increases in the activity of either pyramidal cells or individual ChCs during this temporal window result in a reversible decrease in axo-axonic connections.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We found that increases in the activity of either pyramidal cells or individual ChCs during this temporal window result in a reversible decrease in axo-axonic connections.

findings.claim: By using paired recordings of synaptically connected interneurons in slice preparations of the rat and mouse dentate gyrus (DG), we show that morphologically identified interneurons form complex neuronal networks.
findings.claim_source_sentence: By using paired recordings of synaptically connected interneurons in slice preparations of the rat and mouse dentate gyrus (DG), we show that morphologically identified interneurons form complex neuronal networks.

findings.claim: We found that nearly 100% of PV+ interneurons in adult medial PFC (mPFC) express transcripts encoding GluN1 and GluN2B, and they have functional NMDARs.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We found that nearly 100% of PV+ interneurons in adult medial PFC (mPFC) express transcripts encoding GluN1 and GluN2B, and they have functional NMDARs.

findings.claim: Using this approach, we show that the medial entorhinal cortical alvear inputs onto CA1 pyramidal cells (PCs) and interneurons with cell bodies located in stratum oriens were monosynaptic, had low release probability, and were mediated by glutamate receptors.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Using this approach, we show that the medial entorhinal cortical alvear inputs onto CA1 pyramidal cells (PCs) and interneurons with cell bodies located in stratum oriens were monosynaptic, had low release probability, and were mediated by glutamate receptors.
findings.effect_size: 70% of PV and VIP interneurons and less than 20% of O-LM cells

findings.claim: These results suggest testable hypotheses on the precise effects of GABAergic diversity on cortical computation.
findings.claim_source_sentence: These results suggest testable hypotheses on the precise effects of GABAergic diversity on cortical computation.

findings.claim: Here we tested the role of one cell type in the wakefulness-promoting basal forebrain (BF) region, cortically projecting GABAergic neurons containing PV, whose virally transduced fibers we found apposed cortical PV interneurons involved in generating GBO.

findings.claim: In ASD, the number of PV+ Ch cells is decreased across several cortical areas.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In ASD, the number of PV+ Ch cells is decreased across several cortical areas.

findings.claim: We show that crosshemispheric gamma synchrony strengthens with age, in line with the previously reported increase in local gamma power.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We show that crosshemispheric gamma synchrony strengthens with age, in line with the previously reported increase in local gamma power.

findings.claim: We show that fast rhythmic activity in the prefrontal cortex becomes prominent during the second postnatal week.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We show that fast rhythmic activity in the prefrontal cortex becomes prominent during the second postnatal week.
findings.effect_size: 15 Hz, fast oscillatory activity progressively accelerates with age and stabilizes within gamma frequency range (30-80 Hz) during the fourth postnatal week
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: While initially at about 15 Hz, fast oscillatory activity progressively accelerates with age and stabilizes within gamma frequency range (30-80 Hz) during the fourth postnatal week.

findings.claim: We demonstrate in mice that early postnatal GABAergic signaling between interneurons and oligodendrocyte precursor cells (OPCs) is crucial for myelination of parvalbumin (PV) interneurons, which facilitates in vivo low-gamma oscillations in the medial prefrontal cortex (mPFC) and supports fear extin
findings.claim_source_sentence: We demonstrate in mice that early postnatal GABAergic signaling between interneurons and oligodendrocyte precursor cells (OPCs) is crucial for myelination of parvalbumin (PV) interneurons, which facilitates in vivo low-gamma oscillations in the medial prefrontal cortex (mPFC) and supports fear extinction learning.

findings.claim: These findings demonstrate that phase-dependent amplification of cortical theta power can be induced through targeted stimulation of local excitatory or inhibitory neurons, with the observed phase offset likely reflecting underlying circuit dynamics.
findings.claim_source_sentence: These findings demonstrate that phase-dependent amplification of cortical theta power can be induced through targeted stimulation of local excitatory or inhibitory neurons, with the observed phase offset likely reflecting underlying circuit dynamics.
findings.effect_size: 75Hz) frequency ("gamma" protocol)
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Stimulation was delivered over a quarter of the target theta cycle, either as a single continuous pulse ("continuous" protocol) or three short pulses at gamma (75Hz) frequency ("gamma" protocol).

findings.claim: These findings reveal phase-dependent amplification of cortical theta power can be induced by stimulation of local excitatory or inhibitory neurons, with a phase-offset likely resulting from circuit interactions.

findings.claim: Hence, their dysfunction can lead to hyperexcitability and increased susceptibility to seizures.

findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Interestingly, optogenetic stimulation of parvalbumin interneurons in the mPFC at 40 Hz nested at 8 Hz positively modulated the social behaviors of mice and rescued the social novelty deficit in the KI mice.

findings.claim: Given the link between PV+ interneurons and cognition, in the future, it would be useful to carry out physiological recordings in the PV+ cell type selectively and characterize if and how psychiatric and neurological diseases affect initiation and propagation of electrical signals in this cortical s

findings.effect_size: 80 Hz), which underlie higher cognitive functions

findings.claim: Cntnap2<sup>-/-</sup> mice show a reduction in hippocampal PV interneuron density, reduced inhibitory input to CA1 pyramidal cells, deficits in spatial discrimination ability, and frequency-dependent circuit changes within the hippocampus, including alterations in gamma oscillations, sharp-wave ripp
findings.claim_source_sentence: Cntnap2<sup>-/-</sup> mice show a reduction in hippocampal PV interneuron density, reduced inhibitory input to CA1 pyramidal cells, deficits in spatial discrimination ability, and frequency-dependent circuit changes within the hippocampus, including alterations in gamma oscillations, sharp-wave ripples, and theta-gamma modulation.

findings.claim: This supports the hypothesis that highly energized fast-spiking interneurons are a central element for cortical information processing and may be critical for cognitive decline when energy supply becomes limited ('interneuron energy hypothesis').

findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Gamma oscillations (∼30 to 100 Hz) provide a fundamental mechanism of information processing during sensory perception, motor behavior, and memory formation by coordination of neuronal activity in networks of the hippocampus and neocortex.

findings.claim: We found no difference in PV firing or phase preference during kainate-induced oscillations in younger or older 5xFAD mice compared to control, and a reduction of PV and PNNs only in the oldest 5xFAD mice.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We found no difference in PV firing or phase preference during kainate-induced oscillations in younger or older 5xFAD mice compared to control, and a reduction of PV and PNNs only in the oldest 5xFAD mice.

findings.claim: Evidence suggests that reduced glutamatergic neurotransmission via the N-methyl-D-aspartate (NMDA) receptors that are localized to inhibitory interneurons, perhaps especially the fast-spiking cells that contain the calcium-binding protein parvalbumin (PV), may contribute to gamma band synchrony defi

findings.effect_size: 100 Hz) as a major pathophysiologic feature of schizophrenia
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: A fascinating convergence of evidence in recent years has implicated the disturbances of neural synchrony in the gamma frequency band (30-100 Hz) as a major pathophysiologic feature of schizophrenia.

findings.claim: Our findings reveal a dynamic interplay between pyramidal and fast-spiking interneurons leading to the emergence of gamma activity (∼40 Hz).

findings.claim_source_sentence: Our findings reconcile discrepancies in previous rodent studies, reveal important species differences, and establish a framework for understanding layer-dependent inhibitory computations in the primate cortex.

findings.claim: Here, we show that serotonergic input has separable suppressive effects on the gain of ongoing and evoked visual activity.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here, we show that serotonergic input has separable suppressive effects on the gain of ongoing and evoked visual activity.

findings.claim: Furthermore, SAP97-expressing PV interneurons showed increased glutamatergic input and more extensive dendritic branching when compared with non-expressing PV interneurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Furthermore, SAP97-expressing PV interneurons showed increased glutamatergic input and more extensive dendritic branching when compared with non-expressing PV interneurons.
findings.effect_size: 40%, of adult PV interneurons
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: For PV interneurons in mouse visual cortex, the expression of SAP97 is developmentally regulated, being expressed in almost all juvenile but only a fraction, ~40%, of adult PV interneurons.

findings.claim: They also lead to novel predictions concerning connectivity and network dynamics that can be tested via optogenetic manipulations.
findings.claim_source_sentence: They also lead to novel predictions concerning connectivity and network dynamics that can be tested via optogenetic manipulations.

findings.claim: We show that photoactivation of the 5-HT<sub>2A</sub> receptor pathway in pyramidal neurons enhances firing of both excitatory neurons and interneurons, whereas 5-HT<sub>2A</sub> photoactivation in parvalbumin interneurons produces bidirectional effects.

findings.claim: Here we demonstrate that cortex-projecting basal forebrain parvalbumin-expressing inhibitory neurons (BF-PV) mediate sustained attention in mice performing an attention task.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here we demonstrate that cortex-projecting basal forebrain parvalbumin-expressing inhibitory neurons (BF-PV) mediate sustained attention in mice performing an attention task.

findings.claim: Probing the local field potential activity of the forelimb primary sensory cortex (S1FL) located in the PIC of male adult mice, we found a constant, deep reduction of low-gamma oscillation power (L-gamma; 30-50 Hz) precisely during the critical time window for recovery (1-3 weeks after stroke).

findings.claim: Activating either PV or SOM interneurons produced mixtures of divisive and subtractive effects; threshold nonlinearities interact with network activity to transform the apparent nature of inhibition

findings.effect_size: qualitative mixture of divisive and subtractive in both awake and anesthetized

findings.claim: We found that PV+ cells had stronger and more prevalent on-responses, off-responses, and postresponse suppression compared with presumed pyramidal cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We found that PV+ cells had stronger and more prevalent on-responses, off-responses, and postresponse suppression compared with presumed pyramidal cells.

findings.claim: We show how, in E - PV - SOM recurrently connected networks, SOM-mediated modulation can lead to simultaneous increases in neuronal gain and network stability.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We show how, in E - PV - SOM recurrently connected networks, SOM-mediated modulation can lead to simultaneous increases in neuronal gain and network stability.

findings.claim: Here, we show that changes of GABAergic synaptic input from chandelier cells (ChCs) drive homeostatic tuning of the AIS of principal neurons (PNs) in the prelimbic (PL) region, while those from parvalbumin-positive basket cells do not.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here, we show that changes of GABAergic synaptic input from chandelier cells (ChCs) drive homeostatic tuning of the AIS of principal neurons (PNs) in the prelimbic (PL) region, while those from parvalbumin-positive basket cells do not.

findings.claim: Electron microscopic analysis revealed that PV-IR somata and dendrites are densely innervated by nonimmunoreactive terminals forming asymmetric (Gray type I) synapses as well as by fewer terminals forming symmetric (Gray type II) synapses.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Electron microscopic analysis revealed that PV-IR somata and dendrites are densely innervated by nonimmunoreactive terminals forming asymmetric (Gray type I) synapses as well as by fewer terminals forming symmetric (Gray type II) synapses.

findings.claim: Both LGI2 and FGF13 mutations are implicated in syndromes with epilepsy as a component.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Both LGI2 and FGF13 mutations are implicated in syndromes with epilepsy as a component.

findings.claim: Using en bloc specimens from MTLE subjects with and without hippocampal sclerosis, paired with nonepileptic controls, we demonstrate the utility of this approach for detecting cell-type specific synaptic alterations.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Using en bloc specimens from MTLE subjects with and without hippocampal sclerosis, paired with nonepileptic controls, we demonstrate the utility of this approach for detecting cell-type specific synaptic alterations.

findings.claim: The 5HT3aR group, which also accounts for ∼30% of the total interneuronal population, is heterogeneous and includes all of the neurons that express the neuropeptide VIP, as well as an equally numerous subgroup of neurons that do not express VIP and includes neurogliaform cells.

findings.effect_size: 100% of neocortical interneurons
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Recent data suggest that neurons expressing three markers, the Ca2+-binding protein parvalbumin (PV), the neuropeptide somatostatin (SST), and the ionotropic serotonin receptor 5HT3a (5HT3aR) account for nearly 100% of neocortical interneurons.

findings.claim: Other measures of both parvalbumin- and calretinin-immunoreactive cells were unchanged, including total number of cells, distribution by depth, and sizes of cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Other measures of both parvalbumin- and calretinin-immunoreactive cells were unchanged, including total number of cells, distribution by depth, and sizes of cells.

findings.claim: In this study, we show that each of the three major PV cell classes further splits into functionally distinct sub-classes during fast network events in vivo.

findings.claim: Here we report that a large majority of parvalbumin-expressing (PV+) interneurons in the upper layers of mouse neocortex, from both orbitofrontal and primary somatosensory areas, fire EAPs after sufficient activation of their somata.

findings.claim: We found that this CA1 KA-evoked gamma oscillation was faster than that in CA3 and, crucially, did not appear to require the involvement of fast-spiking basket cells.

findings.claim: Finally, single-cell nuclear RNAsequencing of PV+INTs revealed several misregulated genes related to morphogenesis, cellular excitability, and synapse formation.

findings.effect_size: 80% fast-spiking (FS), 20% non-fast-spiking (NFS)) and four morphological subtypes
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: We find that WT PV+INTs consist of two physiological subtypes (80% fast-spiking (FS), 20% non-fast-spiking (NFS)) and four morphological subtypes.

findings.claim: We also identified members of the cadherin superfamily as candidate regulators of perisomatic inhibition from parvalbumin-expressing basket cells onto deep and superficial excitatory neurons, exerting opposing effects on synapse formation.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We also identified members of the cadherin superfamily as candidate regulators of perisomatic inhibition from parvalbumin-expressing basket cells onto deep and superficial excitatory neurons, exerting opposing effects on synapse formation.

findings.claim: With a revolution in transcriptomics-based cell taxonomy driving the development of novel transgenic mouse lines, selectively monitoring and modulating previously elusive interneuron types is becoming increasingly feasible.
findings.claim_source_sentence: With a revolution in transcriptomics-based cell taxonomy driving the development of novel transgenic mouse lines, selectively monitoring and modulating previously elusive interneuron types is becoming increasingly feasible.

findings.claim: We previously reported that male pups raised by physically active (wheel-running, as opposed to sedentary) dams exhibit tube test dominance and increased reproductive fitness, and here we show that social isolation from weaning also increases dominance.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We previously reported that male pups raised by physically active (wheel-running, as opposed to sedentary) dams exhibit tube test dominance and increased reproductive fitness, and here we show that social isolation from weaning also increases dominance.

findings.claim: These subtypes exhibited reduced intrinsic excitability and a decrease in their synaptic output onto pyramidal neurons, respectively.
findings.claim_source_sentence: These subtypes exhibited reduced intrinsic excitability and a decrease in their synaptic output onto pyramidal neurons, respectively.

findings.claim: We found that while pyramidal cell excitability during Up states significantly increased when SOM cells were optogenetically silenced, VIP cells did not influence pyramidal cell excitability either upon optogenetic silencing or activation.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We found that while pyramidal cell excitability during Up states significantly increased when SOM cells were optogenetically silenced, VIP cells did not influence pyramidal cell excitability either upon optogenetic silencing or activation.

findings.claim: Here we show that excitatory inputs to major archetypal classes of neocortical inhibitory neurons, fast-spiking (FS) and non-fast-spiking (non-FS), express a novel type of plasticity, weight-dependent heterosynaptic plasticity, which accompanies the induction of Hebbian-type changes.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here we show that excitatory inputs to major archetypal classes of neocortical inhibitory neurons, fast-spiking (FS) and non-fast-spiking (non-FS), express a novel type of plasticity, weight-dependent heterosynaptic plasticity, which accompanies the induction of Hebbian-type changes.

findings.claim: Immunohistochemical analysis revealed that Cav3.1 was predominantly localized in PV-positive GABAergic neurons in the mPFC.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Immunohistochemical analysis revealed that Cav3.1 was predominantly localized in PV-positive GABAergic neurons in the mPFC.

findings.claim: These morphologically distinct SOM<sup>+</sup> interneurons corresponded to different transcriptomic cell types and were differentially integrated into the local circuit with only S1 neurons receiving local excitatory input.

findings.claim: We demonstrate a novel viral gene expression strategy to target cells with specific miRNA expression using miRNA-guided neuron tags (mAGNET).
findings.claim_source_sentence: We demonstrate a novel viral gene expression strategy to target cells with specific miRNA expression using miRNA-guided neuron tags (mAGNET).

findings.claim: Through optogenetic manipulation of PV and SST interneurons and computational modeling of the hippocampal neural circuits, we dissected the contributions of PV and SST interneuron circuit dysfunctions on AβO<sub>1-42</sub>-induced impairments of hippocampal theta-nested gamma oscillations and oscill
findings.claim_source_sentence:
Through optogenetic manipulation of PV and SST interneurons and computational modeling of the hippocampal neural circuits, we dissected the contributions of PV and SST interneuron circuit dysfunctions on AβO1-42-induced impairments of hippocampal theta-nested gamma oscillations and oscillation-induced LTP.

Results

Targeted whole-cell patch-clamp recordings and optogenetic manipulations of PV and SST interneurons during in vivo-like, optogenetically induced theta-nested gamma oscillations in vitro revealed that AβO1-42 causes synapse-specific dysfunction in PV and SST interneurons.

findings.claim: The results are discussed in the context of the role of PV interneurons in circuit function and how PNN removal alters this function.

findings.claim: Using subtype-selective blockers for GABA<sub>A</sub> receptors, we show that dendrite-targeting somatostatin interneurons and NO-synthase-positive neurogliaform cells preferentially activate α5-subunit- containing GABA<sub>A</sub> receptors (α5-GABA<sub>A</sub>Rs), generating slow inhibitory postsy

findings.claim_source_sentence: We show that inhibitory axo-somatic synapses onto pyramidal neurons vary from one cortical layer to another; however, inhibitory synapses on axon initial segments (AISs) were similar across layers.

findings.claim: PV neurons principally divide responses and preserve stimulus selectivity, whereas SOM neurons principally subtract from excitatory responses and sharpen selectivity in visual cortex in vivo
findings.claim_source_sentence: We find that soma-targeting, parvalbumin-expressing (PV) neurons principally divide responses but preserve stimulus selectivity, whereas dendrite-targeting, somatostatin-expressing (SOM) neurons principally subtract from excitatory responses and sharpen selectivity.
findings.effect_size: PV activation: 28.5 ± 4.5% reduction at peak, 11 ± 4% decrease in baseline; SOM activation: 19.8 ± 11.2% and 19 ± 6% respectively
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: +PV (PV neurons activated optically), 11 ± 4% decrease in baseline, P < 0.05, n = 52 cells; versus +SOM, 19 ± 6%, P < 0.001, n = 25 cells

findings.claim: PV cell impact on pyramidal cells is captured by a linear transformation, both additive and multiplicative with a threshold, indicating PV cells modulate cortical gain
findings.claim_source_sentence: PV cells' impact on pyramidal cells is captured by a linear transformation, both additive and multiplicative, with a threshold. These results indicate that PV cells are ideally suited to modulate cortical gain and establish a causal relationship between a select neuron type and specific computations performed by the cortex during sensory processing.
findings.effect_size: linear transformation with additive and multiplicative components
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: PV cells' impact on pyramidal cells is captured by a linear transformation, both additive and multiplicative, with a threshold.

findings.claim_source_sentence: we found that elevated spiking of PV+ interneurons markedly sharpened orientation tuning and enhanced direction selectivity of nearby neurons
findings.effect_size: significant sharpening of orientation tuning and enhanced direction selectivity
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: we found that elevated spiking of PV+ interneurons markedly sharpened orientation tuning and enhanced direction selectivity of nearby neurons

findings.claim: Light-driven activation of fast-spiking interneurons at varied frequencies selectively amplifies gamma oscillations (20-80 Hz range), while pyramidal neuron activation amplifies only lower frequency oscillations
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here we show that light-driven activation of fast-spiking interneurons at varied frequencies (8–200 Hz) selectively amplifies gamma oscillations. In contrast, pyramidal neuron activation amplifies only lower frequency oscillations, a cell-type-specific double dissociation.

findings.claim: Inhibiting PV interneurons suppresses gamma oscillations in vivo, while driving PV interneurons generates emergent gamma-frequency rhythmicity; gamma modulation enhances information transmission
findings.claim_source_sentence: inhibiting parvalbumin interneurons suppresses gamma oscillations in vivo, whereas driving these interneurons (even by means of non-rhythmic principal cell activity) is sufficient to generate emergent gamma-frequency rhythmicity
findings.effect_size: gamma power reduced 20 ± 4% by PV inhibition (P<0.001); information increased from 0.43 ± 0.05 to 0.62 ± 0.04 bits (P<0.001)
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: gamma modulation increased the amount of information that spike rates transmitted about the number of sEPSCs over gamma-cycle time windows (non-rhythmic, 0.43 ± 0.05 bits; gamma, 0.62 ± 0.04 bits; P < 0.001)

conflicts.description: The 2012 gain control controversy: Whether PV interneuron activation produces divisive (gain-scaling) or subtractive (additive shift) effects on cortical responses
conflicts.paper_a_claim: PV neurons principally divide responses and preserve stimulus selectivity; divisive gain control by PV cells
conflicts.paper_a_source_sentence: We find that soma-targeting, parvalbumin-expressing (PV) neurons principally divide responses but preserve stimulus selectivity
conflicts.paper_b_claim: PV cell impact on pyramidal cells is a linear transformation with both additive and multiplicative components with a threshold - not purely divisive
conflicts.paper_b_source_sentence: PV cells' impact on pyramidal cells is captured by a linear transformation, both additive and multiplicative, with a threshold.
conflicts.resolution_notes: The apparent discrepancy was largely explained by differences in analysis approach and by threshold nonlinearities. A key resolution came from studies showing that the same inhibition can appear divisive or subtractive depending on cellular properties and network state.

conflicts.description: Whether PV activation sharpens orientation tuning (Lee 2012) or has little effect on tuning (Atallah 2012; Wilson 2012)
conflicts.paper_a_claim: PV+ interneuron activation markedly sharpened orientation tuning and enhanced direction selectivity
conflicts.paper_a_source_sentence: we found that elevated spiking of PV+ interneurons markedly sharpened orientation tuning and enhanced direction selectivity of nearby neurons
conflicts.paper_b_claim: PV activation preserves stimulus selectivity while dividing responses; tuning width was not significantly altered
conflicts.paper_b_source_sentence: control, half-width at half-height, 38.7 ± 3.0 degrees; versus +PV, 35.2 ±3.0 deg; P = 0.31
conflicts.resolution_notes: Differences attributed to stimulus paradigm differences, analysis methods (tuning width vs selectivity index), and potentially different levels of PV activation. Studies with intermediate activation levels suggest PV effects on selectivity are stimulus-dependent.

conflicts.description: Whether PV and SOM interneuron types have cleanly separable computational functions (divisive vs subtractive) or whether these effects depend on network context
conflicts.paper_a_claim: PV cells divide, SOM cells subtract - clean functional dissociation between inhibitory subtypes
conflicts.paper_a_source_sentence: soma-targeting, parvalbumin-expressing (PV) neurons principally divide responses but preserve stimulus selectivity, whereas dendrite-targeting, somatostatin-expressing (SOM) neurons principally subtract from excitatory responses
conflicts.paper_b_claim: Both PV and SOM activation produce mixtures of divisive and subtractive effects that depend on network configuration and cellular properties
conflicts.paper_b_source_sentence: activating either family of interneurons produced mixtures of divisive and subtractive effects and that simultaneously recorded neurons were often suppressed in qualitatively different ways
conflicts.resolution_notes: The clean PV-divisive/SOM-subtractive dichotomy is likely an oversimplification. Network state, threshold effects, and cellular heterogeneity all contribute to the observed computation.

conflicts.description: Whether PV interneurons are necessary and sufficient for cortical gamma oscillations, or whether other mechanisms (including SOM interneurons) can also generate gamma
conflicts.paper_a_claim: Driving fast-spiking PV cells selectively amplifies gamma oscillations; gamma is a resonant circuit property of FS-mediated inhibition
conflicts.paper_a_source_sentence: Here we show that light-driven activation of fast-spiking interneurons at varied frequencies (8–200 Hz) selectively amplifies gamma oscillations.
conflicts.paper_b_claim: Somatostatin interneurons are critical for context-dependent gamma rhythm in visual cortex; PV interneurons are not the only mechanism
conflicts.paper_b_source_sentence: dendrite-targeting somatostatin (SOM) interneurons are critical for a visually induced, context-dependent gamma rhythm in the visual cortex (V1)
conflicts.resolution_notes: Multiple gamma-generating mechanisms likely coexist. PV-mediated perisomatic inhibition drives one class of gamma oscillations (ING/PING models), while SOM-mediated dendritic inhibition can generate context-dependent gamma. These may serve different computational functions.

conflicts.description: Whether chandelier cell axo-axonic synapses are inhibitory (conventional view) or can be excitatory/depolarizing under some conditions
conflicts.paper_a_claim: GABAergic axo-axonic cells can have excitatory effects in cortical microcircuits
conflicts.paper_a_source_sentence: Excitatory effect of GABAergic axo-axonic cells in cortical microcircuits.
conflicts.paper_b_claim: Depolarizing effect of neocortical chandelier neurons is confirmed but functional impact depends on conditions
conflicts.paper_b_source_sentence: Depolarizing effect of neocortical chandelier neurons.
conflicts.resolution_notes: Chandelier cell effects depend on the chloride reversal potential at the axon initial segment, which differs from the soma. The depolarizing GABA effect at the AIS may serve as shunting inhibition functionally even if it shifts membrane potential positively.

conflicts.description: Whether PV-Cre genetic tools adequately separate basket and chandelier cell functions, given that both express PV
conflicts.paper_a_claim: PV-Cre driver lines label both basket cells and chandelier cells, providing tools for manipulating PV interneurons
conflicts.paper_a_source_sentence: A resource of Cre driver lines for genetic targeting of GABAergic neurons in cerebral cortex.
conflicts.paper_b_claim: Cortical control of chandelier cells may be functionally distinct from basket cells, creating a confound in PV-Cre experiments
conflicts.paper_b_source_sentence: Cortical control of chandelier cells in neural codes.
conflicts.resolution_notes: The basket-chandelier confound remains unresolved in most PV-Cre manipulation studies. Chandelier cells represent ~10-15% of PV+ neurons and target axon initial segments, making their effects qualitatively different from basket cell perisomatic inhibition.

figure_data.homogeneity_check: Mixed comparability: All studies use optogenetic PV activation in mouse cortex, but differ in cortical area (V1 vs auditory), recording method (two-photon vs extracellular vs multiunit), behavioral state (awake vs anesthetized), and stimulus paradigm. These methodological differences likely explain the apparent discrepancy in results.

The SOM-gamma finding specifically challenges the universality of the PV-gamma link by showing context-dependent gamma can rely on different mechanisms.
